hero.title: Tomodachi Life 위키

tomodachiLifePersonalityGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Personality Guide
tomodachiLifePersonalityGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life uses four editable traits during Mii creation to place each resident into one of 16 personalities. Grouped into Easygoing, Outgoing, Independent, and Confident, personalities affect social chemistry, dialogue flavor, and key social outcomes on your island.

steps.title: How the personality quiz works
steps.description: The personality chart is built from Movement, Speech, Expressiveness, and Attitude. The Overall row is cosmetic and does not change the final personality result. Tomodachi Life keeps the same 16 personality types used in Tomodachi Collection.

steps.title: Easygoing personalities
steps.description: Types: Softie, Optimist, Buddy, Dreamer. Group tone: relaxed, open-minded, empathetic. Softie is emotional and highly empathetic. Optimist stays upbeat. Buddy puts friends first. Dreamer is idealistic and romantic.

steps.title: Outgoing personalities
steps.description: Types: Trendsetter, Entertainer, Charmer, Leader. Group tone: social, charming, energetic. Trendsetter is stylish and socially adaptable. Entertainer loves attention. Charmer gets along with almost anyone. Leader is assertive and tends to take charge.

steps.title: Independent personalities
steps.description: Types: Free Spirit, Artist, Lone Wolf, Thinker. Group tone: self-sufficient, individual, introspective. Free Spirit follows personal values. Artist is quiet and creative. Lone Wolf keeps emotions private. Thinker is reflective and analytical.

steps.title: Confident personalities
steps.description: Types: Designer, Adventurer, Brainiac, Go-Getter. Group tone: organized, motivated, focused. Designer is polished and goal-oriented. Adventurer seeks new challenges. Brainiac is highly intelligent. Go-Getter pushes ahead with confidence.

quickTips: Miis with similar personalities tend to like each other more.
A personality profile becomes viewable once your island has at least one resident with that personality.
Personalities affect dialogue flavor, social chemistry, and some romance reactions.
You can check a Mii's personality group in their profile at any time.

tomodachiLifeQRCodes.title: Tomodachi Life QR Codes
tomodachiLifeQRCodes.intro: QR codes let you add established characters, community creations, and ready-made Miis without rebuilding them by hand. The best QR resources combine large Mii archives with clear import instructions and curated packs you can scan immediately.

cards.name: Official Mii Archive on MiiWiki
cards.description: MiiWiki's QR code category is an official-created Mii archive with over 1,015 total QR files, including Aerith, Aonuma, Barret, and many more. Best for broad discovery when you want a large back catalog.

cards.name: Nintendo Life Team Pack
cards.description: Nintendo Life published a ready-to-scan staff pack for Tomodachi Life, generated inside the game itself. Carries over clothing, room décor, catchphrases, personality traits, voice, and original-island information.

cards.name: Crystal Dreams QR Collection
cards.description: Crystal Dreams keeps a long-running Tomodachi Life QR page that works well as a community browse source. Especially useful when you want fan-made picks instead of a strict official archive.

cards.name: QR Import Flow
cards.description: In Mii Maker: open QR Code/Image Options, tap Scan QR Code, align the code, then save the Mii. In Tomodachi Life: go to Town Hall, choose New Mii, then transfer the saved Mii from Mii Maker.

milestones: Scan your first Mii QR code

tomodachiLifeBeginnerGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Beginner Guide
tomodachiLifeBeginnerGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life feels random at first, but the early game has a readable loop: add residents, solve problem bubbles, unlock facilities, level up Miis, and let relationships snowball into bigger events.

items.name: Start with a balanced resident pool
items.description: Create your first Miis and keep expanding early. Aim for at least 1 male and 1 female Mii quickly. Many early buildings require a gender mix, so a balanced cast speeds up progress.

items.name: Solve black-bubble problems first
items.description: Problem bubbles are the main progression engine. At 5 solved problems with 1 male + 1 female Mii, the Clothing Store opens. At 10 problems with 2 male + 2 female, the Hat Store opens.

items.name: Use money and shops to stabilize
items.description: Shops give you more ways to raise happiness, solve requests, and shape each resident. Save money early so the Interiors Store opens after two Miis save $200 or more.

items.name: Level up Miis as soon as possible
items.description: Every level-up unlocks rewards: songs open the Concert Hall, gifts open the Observation Tower. Each reward deepens your island's sandbox and creates new social events.

items.name: Push into island-wide systems
items.description: At 5 Miis and 15 problems: Rankings Board opens. At 7 Miis with 3+ of each gender: Mii News opens. At 2+2 Miis and 30 problems: Compatibility Tester unlocks.

items.name: Let friendships turn into romance
items.description: A love confession unlocks the Park and Café. A wedding unlocks Mii Homes. Keep solving relationship problems to create more chances for confessions, dates, and marriages.

tomodachiLifeCompatibilityGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Compatibility Guide
tomodachiLifeCompatibilityGuide.intro: Compatibility in Tomodachi Life is part forecast tool and part relationship planner. It gives you a fast way to see who is likely to mesh well, which pairs qualify for romance checks, and what status ladder a relationship can climb.

solutions.name: Compatibility Tester
solutions.description: Unlocks when you have 2 male Miis, 2 female Miis, and 30 solved problems. This is the first real tool for planning friendships and romance instead of waiting blindly.

solutions.name: Birthday Requirement
solutions.description: Both Miis need full birthdays saved in their profile. Without full birthdays, compatibility cannot be tested. Set them early for residents you care about.

solutions.name: Friendship Forecast
solutions.description: Used for same-gender pairs, family pairs, or different age group opposite-gender pairs. Shows the next week's Work/Study, Chats, and Leisure outlook.

solutions.name: Romance Forecast
solutions.description: Used for opposite-gender pairs in the same age group. Adds the Love forecast alongside other weekly categories. Best for choosing which pairings to push toward confessions and marriage.

managementTips: Forecast scale runs from full sun (best) to full storm (worst).
Miis with similar personalities tend to like each other more.
Travel tickets can raise a relationship by one or two levels automatically.
A rejected confession can drop both sides by as many as three levels.
Check compatibility before pushing romance — failed confessions are costly.

tomodachiLifeRelationshipGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Relationship Guide
tomodachiLifeRelationshipGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life runs on hidden social momentum. Every friendship, romance, and family bond sits on a visible status ladder, and those ladders directly affect whether Miis become best friends, start dating, propose, fight, or break up. The fastest way to manage island drama is to learn which statuses matter, which actions raise them, and which mistakes make them collapse.

cards.name: Relationship Categories
cards.description: The game tracks seven main categories: Spouse, Family, Siblings, Sweetheart, Best Friend, Friends, and Ex-Sweethearts/Ex-Spouses. Each category uses its own wording for relationship strength, and stronger bonds sit toward green while weaker bonds sit toward purple.

cards.name: Friendship Ladder
cards.description: For Friends, Family, and Siblings in the NA version, the ladder runs from strongest to weakest: Best bud, Good buddy, Great pal, Good pal, Getting along OK, Kinda getting along, Not getting along. If a pair slides into the bottom, fights become more dangerous.

cards.name: Romance and Marriage Signals
cards.description: Strong Sweetheart statuses include Let's get married!, Super in love, Totally in love, Very much in love. Strong Spouse statuses include Soul mate, Super happy, Totally happy, Very happy. These are the status words to check before expecting a confession or proposal.

cards.name: Ways to Raise a Relationship
cards.description: The safest ways to improve bonds are positive relationship advice, dates, and travel tickets. If a Mii asks what you think of a relationship, saying they are great together raises the status. A travel ticket vacation raises the pair by one or two levels automatically.

cards.name: How Relationships Break Down
cards.description: Arguments, failed reconciliations, and rejected confessions can damage bonds quickly. If a conversation goes badly it can turn into an argument, and if a fight is not resolved the pair can revert to acquaintances. A rejected confession can drop both sides by as many as three levels.

cards.name: Managing Breakup Risk
cards.description: If a sweetheart asks to break up and you tell them to work things out, a successful reconciliation resets a negative dating relationship back to its default level. This recovery rule applies to sweethearts with negative status, not spouses asking for divorce.

highlights: Tell worried Miis they are great together
Use travel tickets to boost bond levels
Resolve arguments before they escalate into breakups
Rejected confessions drop both sides by up to three levels

tomodachiLifeMarriageGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Marriage Guide
tomodachiLifeMarriageGuide.intro: Marriage in Tomodachi Life is not a random cutscene you simply wait for. First, a dating pair has to stay stable long enough to trigger a proposal request. Then you must help with a timing-based proposal minigame. Once the proposal succeeds, the couple moves into Mii Homes, unlocks married life, and can eventually ask for a baby.

steps.name: Keep the couple stable long enough
steps.description: A marriage request usually appears after the pair has been together for some time and both Miis keep a good relationship with each other.
facts: Proposal timing can range from months to, very rarely, under an hour.
Very rarely, a proposal request can appear even when the couple is still at the default dating level.

steps.name: Accept the proposal request
steps.description: When one sweetheart gets a love problem and says they want to propose, choose to help instead of putting it off.
facts: Saying Not right now does not instantly fail the relationship, but the request can disappear if you wait too long.
The proposing Mii asks directly whether they should propose.

steps.name: Choose the proposal setup
steps.description: Before the minigame starts, you choose where the proposal happens and whether the proposer should change clothes.
facts: The four proposal locations are Fireworks show, Train station, Amusement park, and Fancy restaurant.
Each location has its own interruptions that can break the timing flow.

steps.name: Win the proposal minigame
steps.description: During the proposal, tap the heart button only when the partner is thinking about the proposer.
facts: You must get the timing right four times.
If you make three mistakes, the proposal fails.
Thought bubbles can switch quickly, so fast, clean timing matters more than tapping often.

steps.name: Handle child couples correctly
steps.description: If the couple are kids, they cannot complete the wedding unless you use an Age-o-matic.
facts: After a successful proposal, a kid couple receives an Age-o-matic as the gift.
If you refuse to use it, the wedding is called off.

steps.name: Watch the wedding and post-marriage move
steps.description: A successful proposal leads to the wedding, then a honeymoon, then a move into Mii Homes.
facts: After marriage, the couple spends their honeymoon in a random country.
They return with a souvenir and settle in a newly built house.
They can switch between being shown in their house and apartments.

steps.name: Unlock married-life outcomes
steps.description: Marriage opens the long-term family layer of the game, but it does not make the relationship permanent.
facts: A married couple can later ask for a baby if the baby setting is enabled.
Divorce is still possible after marriage.

tomodachiLifeBabyGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Baby Guide
tomodachiLifeBabyGuide.intro: Babies only appear after a couple is already married and living in Mii Homes. Once that condition is met, the player controls whether births are allowed, whether a specific request is accepted, how the baby is customized, how the child is raised, and whether they stay on the island or leave as a traveler.

steps.name: Turn baby births on and keep the marriage positive
steps.description: Babies can only happen if baby births are enabled in Town Hall and the married couple has a positive relationship.
facts: The request appears as a love problem from one member of the married couple.
A couple must already be living in Mii Homes before they can have a child.

steps.name: Accept the baby request
steps.description: When a spouse says they have been thinking about having a baby, you can approve or decline.
facts: If approved, the baby arrives about one to two days later.
A married couple can have multiple children, but can only actively raise one child at home at a time.
If you disable baby births before the delivery, the pending birth is cancelled.

steps.name: Customize the newborn
steps.description: At birth, the mother asks a short series of questions that decide the baby's first profile settings.
facts: You can choose Boy, Girl, or let the gender be random.
The baby's default appearance is a mix of the mother and father's features.
You can accept the suggested name, ask for another one, or enter your own.
The baby's personality can be set like Dad, like Mom, or random.

steps.name: Follow the six-day growth cycle
steps.description: Children grow in visible daily stages and become ready to leave home on day six.
facts: Day 1: newborn in arms, bassinet, or stroller on outings.
Day 2: crawling begins.
Day 3: hair starts growing and the baby can stand briefly.
Day 4: toddler stage begins and walking practice starts.
Day 5: the child walks, runs, and speaks in a higher-pitched voice.
Day 6: the child is fully grown and ready to move out.

steps.name: Use the right babysitting actions by age
steps.description: Babysitting changes depending on the child's current stage.
facts: Day 1 uses rhythmic rocking with the 3DS system.
Days 2 to 3 use head pats, belly tickles, and peekaboo.
Days 4 to 5 use circular stylus motions to lift and spin the child in the backyard.
Parents only call for crying-baby help during the first three days, and they do not call after 8:00 PM.

steps.name: Choose resident or traveler on day six
steps.description: Once the child grows up, the parents immediately call you to decide the next life path.
facts: Making the child a resident moves them into their own apartment on the island.
Sending the child away turns them into a traveler who waits at the port for StreetPass boats.
In both cases, you get one more chance to edit the child's profile before they leave home.

steps.name: Know the family restrictions and records
steps.description: The child system temporarily changes what the game lets you do with the family.
facts: Once the baby is born, neither parent can be deleted until the child grows up and moves out.
Married Miis do not fight or divorce while they are actively raising a child at home.
The family album is stored under Child Info in Town Hall, which can hold up to 50 albums.

tomodachiLifeFoodGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Food Guide
tomodachiLifeFoodGuide.intro: Food is one of the most repeated systems in Tomodachi Life. It levels Miis, fills hunger, reveals favorite and hated foods, cures certain problems, and supports completion goals through the Town Hall collection list. The smartest way to shop is to understand the Food Mart rotation first, then learn which categories and reaction tiers are worth chasing.

items.name: Food Mart Basics
items.description: The Food Mart is the first shop unlocked in the game. It opens after the first Mii asks for food, and its stock rotates daily.
items.playerUse: Check it every day early, because new purchases permanently expand your buy-again stock.

items.name: Daily Specials
items.description: Each day the shop offers 5 Daily Specials that are randomly selected from foods you have not bought yet. Once purchased, it is added to the regular In Stock list.
items.playerUse: This is the cleanest way to grow your catalog without waiting for random gifts.

items.name: Food Categories
items.description: Main dishes: 43 total and fill hunger the most. Side dishes: 83 total and fill less than mains. Desserts: 81 total. Beverages: 24 total and fill the least hunger.
items.playerUse: Use mains when hunger is low, and keep drinks or lighter foods for smaller top-ups.

items.name: Reaction Tiers
items.description: Super All-Time Favorite launches the Mii into space. All-Time Favorite triggers a dance sequence. Worst causes a major happiness drop. Worst Ever empties the happiness bar completely.
items.playerUse: These four reactions are the key milestones for food collecting and mood control.

items.name: Mood Recovery Value
items.description: A Mii's two strongest favorite-food tiers instantly cheer up a sad Mii and can also be used instead of cold medicine to cure a cold.
items.playerUse: Once you know a Mii's best foods, keep them in reserve for emergencies instead of feeding them casually.

items.name: Special Foods
items.description: Special foods always trigger a very positive response and award the largest amount of money and happiness. They never appear in a Mii's favorite-food list and can never become All-Time Favorite or Worst Ever.
items.playerUse: Use these when you want guaranteed upside instead of gambling on hidden preferences.

items.name: Trash Food
items.description: Trash food always costs $0.20 and is guaranteed to be disliked. It cannot be bought from the Food Mart. The Tomodachi Life trash foods are: Ruined meal, Moldy Bread, Banana Peel, and Spoiled Milk.
items.playerUse: Treat these as collection curiosities, not practical food. They are useful only for filling out item lists or chasing reaction data.

unlockMilestones: Find a Super All-Time Favorite reaction
Complete a food category in the collection
Stock a Mii's top favorite as an emergency cheer-up

tomodachiLifeGiftsGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Gifts Guide
tomodachiLifeGiftsGuide.intro: In Tomodachi Life, level-up gifts differ from normal consumable gifts. A Mii can own up to 8 level-up gifts at once, and non-Japanese versions include 28 total options. Some gifts create apartment scenes, others drive group activities, outdoor variety, or conflict recovery.

sections.name: How the gift system works
sections.description: Every level-up can reward a gift. A single Mii holds up to 8 gifts; adding a ninth means replacing one existing gift. Miis usually try a newly assigned gift right after you leave their apartment.

sections.name: Best gifts for repeat apartment scenes
sections.description: Beauty Kit, Laptop, Mirror, and Study Kit create frequent solo scenes indoors. The Laptop can attract up to 3 visitors and sometimes appears to crash. Beauty Kit temporarily changes a Mii's eye appearance.

sections.name: Best gifts for group moments
sections.description: CD gathers up to 4 Miis and only the owner needs it. Wii U draws up to 4 with distinct win and loss reactions. Rent-a-Cat and Rent-a-Dog Coupons bring pets that draw visitors into the apartment.

sections.name: Best gifts for outdoor variety
sections.description: Guitar plays different tunes in the apartment or at the fountain. Fishing Pole sends a Mii out on a rowing boat. Metal Detector finds coins or trash on the beach. Soccer Ball, Skateboard, and Baseball Bat add map activity.

sections.name: Best gift for fight recovery
sections.description: Punching Bag is the only level-up gift angry Miis can use after a fight. It appears more often when a Mii is upset, making it the most practical pick for islands with many rivalries or frequent big fights.

sections.name: Easy gift strategy
sections.description: Choose CD, Wii U, or Rent-a-Cat Coupon for social islands. Choose Beauty Kit, Mirror, or Laptop for personality-heavy apartments. Keep at least one Punching Bag in rotation for smoother conflict recovery.

growthMilestones: Give a Mii their first level-up gift
Unlock the Observation Tower with a gift reward
Use a Punching Bag to calm a fight-angry Mii

tomodachiLifeClothingAndHats.title: Tomodachi Life Clothing and Hats Guide
tomodachiLifeClothingAndHats.intro: Tomodachi Life separates full outfits from headwear. Clothing is purchased in the Clothing Shop and hats in the Hats Shop, with daily rotations, seasonal stock, and permanent catalog access once acquired. Clothing totals 428 item types and 3,341 colors; hats total 157 items and 1,111 colors.

priorities.name: Clothing Shop Unlock
priorities.description: Opens with 1 male + 1 female resident and 5 solved problems. Daily Specials refresh with up to 5 random outfits each day. Seasonal clothing rotates every 3 months. Acquired items stay permanently available.

priorities.name: Hats Shop Unlock
priorities.description: Opens with 2 male + 2 female residents and 10 solved problems. Shows 3 different hats daily in Daily Specials. Bought hats stay available forever under In Stock. Seasonal hats also rotate every 3 months.

priorities.name: Clothing Categories
priorities.description: 428 items total: Masculine (75), Feminine (109), Unisex (69), Formal Wear (69), Costumes (87). Once an outfit or color is acquired it remains permanently available in the catalog.

priorities.name: Hat Categories
priorities.description: 157 items total: Regular hats (69), Costume hats (32), Hair accessories (56). Hats and outfits are separate layers — one hat can pair with many outfit combinations.

priorities.name: Clothing and Hat Reactions
priorities.description: Loved it: Mii spins in the air. Really likes it: jumps. Likes it: hands on hips. Doesn't like much: tilts head. Doesn't like at all: looks away. Unlike food, no super-favorite or worst-ever reactions apply.

priorities.name: Import Wear and Seasonal Stock
priorities.description: Rare style unlocks come from special events, space travel, and Import Wear via StreetPass. Arriving traveler boats can bring exclusive outfits and hats from other islands into your catalog.

tomodachiLifeInteriorsGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Interiors Guide
tomodachiLifeInteriorsGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life includes 102 interior designs in the main release. The Interiors shop unlocks once you have at least $200 in pocket money, and six level-up-only interiors cannot be purchased normally: Family, Modern Asian, Natural Wood, Plant, Polka Dot, and Tiled.

achievements.title: Family
achievements.description: An ordinary family-style room. Exclusive to level-up rewards. Cannot be purchased from the normal Interiors shop.

achievements.title: Modern Asian
achievements.description: A clean, stylish room built around a shoes-off, carefully kept look. Exclusive to level-up rewards.

achievements.title: Natural Wood
achievements.description: A soothing wood-focused room, warm and timeless. Works well for relaxed or nature-themed islands.

achievements.title: Plant
achievements.description: A bright plant-heavy room built around greenery and an airy atmosphere. Good for cheerful, easygoing Miis.

achievements.title: Polka Dot
achievements.description: A lively room with bright polka-dot energy. Great for comedy islands and bold room variety.

achievements.title: Tiled
achievements.description: A tile-focused room with a distinctive clean pattern and strong visual identity. Exclusive to level-up rewards.

achievements.title: Fairy-tale Castle ($500)
achievements.description: A magical, high-concept room that turns an apartment into a fantasy castle scene. One of the most visually dramatic interiors.

achievements.title: How to Unlock the Interiors Shop
achievements.description: The Interiors Shop unlocks once you have at least $200 in pocket money across any two Miis.

tomodachiLifeTravelersAndStreetPass.title: Tomodachi Life Travelers and StreetPass Guide
tomodachiLifeTravelersAndStreetPass.intro: Travelers are special Miis that move between islands through StreetPass. The Port handles departures and arrivals, while the Campground is where incoming travelers stay for the night.

faqs.question: How do I create my own traveler in Tomodachi Life?
faqs.answer: Two married Miis must have a baby. Once that child grows up on day six, choose to send them away to travel instead of making them a resident. StreetPass must be enabled on both the system and for Tomodachi Life.

faqs.question: How do the Port and Campground unlock in Tomodachi Life?
faqs.answer: The Port unlocks after at least 1 StreetPass or after sending someone to travel. The Campground unlocks when you send or receive a traveler. The Campground can hold up to 10 tents at once.

faqs.question: What happens after a Tomodachi Life StreetPass tag?
faqs.answer: Each StreetPass can send 1 traveler from your Port queue. If the other island also has a traveler waiting, 1 can arrive on your island. The Port can report up to 10 arriving boats at a time and the dock holds up to 50 travelers total.

faqs.question: How do I find and help a newly arrived Tomodachi Life traveler?
faqs.answer: New travelers may appear at the fountain, park, observation tower, beach, café, or amusement park. Accept their request to camp. At 4:00 PM they retire to the Campground and set up a tent.

faqs.question: What problems can Tomodachi Life campground travelers have?
faqs.answer: Dirty travelers need a bath set. Homesick travelers need a music box or head pat. Hungry travelers need food or drinks. Stomach bugs need stomach medicine. Hot head needs cold medicine. Solved problems can earn special food for 1 to 5 Play Coins.

faqs.question: What rewards do Tomodachi Life travelers leave behind?
faqs.answer: Well-helped travelers may leave a letter and a treasure gift. Possible gifts include tissues, copper coin, silver coin, and gold coin. Arriving boats can also bring another island's Import Wear exclusive outfits and hats.

tomodachiLifeImportWear.title: Tomodachi Life Import Wear Guide
tomodachiLifeImportWear.subtitle: Track how Import Wear opens, how export clothing is chosen, and how StreetPass and SpotPass shaped the rare clothing loop.
tomodachiLifeImportWear.intro: Import Wear is the island's special import shop. Instead of a simple daily rotation, it preserves imported stock from StreetPass exchanges and historical SpotPass distributions, which is why it matters most for completionists.

items.details: Import Wear opens when you first receive clothing through StreetPass or SpotPass, or when you first choose your island's export clothing.
items.playerTakeaway: You usually enter the import loop as soon as you start using StreetPass or finish the export setup tied to the Hats shop.

items.details: Import Wear stocks imported clothing and hats brought in from other islands through StreetPass and from official SpotPass distribution.
items.playerTakeaway: This is the shop to watch if you want outfits and headwear that do not come from the normal shop rotation.

items.details: After the Hats shop opens, the export quiz offers three clothing options. You may retake the quiz one time, but once one export item is chosen, it cannot be changed.
items.playerTakeaway: Your island only gets one permanent export outfit choice per save, so completion-focused players should decide carefully before locking it in.

items.details: Your chosen export outfit travels to other islands through StreetPass and stays in their Import Wear catalog forever. Imported items you receive are added as permanent shop stock once obtained.
items.playerTakeaway: Even if you do not buy an imported item immediately, getting it into the shop catalog is the important part.

items.details: 24 SpotPass items were documented historically, originally rotating on half-month intervals. When the same item returned the following year, remaining colors were added.
items.playerTakeaway: Full SpotPass-era completion originally required long-term play across repeated distribution windows.

items.details: SpotPass ended on April 8, 2024. The Schoolgirl Uniform is the remaining SpotPass-era item still referenced in community documentation. Final regional import outfits for Japan, Korea, and PAL regions were all intended for female Miis.
items.playerTakeaway: Modern completion runs rely on what is already stocked on a save file and on StreetPass-traded imports from other players.

tomodachiLifeConcertHall.title: Tomodachi Life Concert Hall Songs
tomodachiLifeConcertHall.subtitle: Learn every song style, how lyric editing works, and how solo and group performances are built.
tomodachiLifeConcertHall.intro: The Concert Hall turns leveled-up Miis into performers. Once a Mii learns a song genre, you can rewrite lyrics, change outfits, build groups, and turn ordinary islanders into full comedy acts or parody bands.

items.heading: Song Types and Default Songs
items.summary: International versions have a larger song set than the Japanese release.
points: 8 song types in NTSC, PAL, and Korean versions: Metal, Pop, Rock & Roll, Rap, Ballad, Opera, Techno, and Musical.
6 song types in the Japanese version: Metal, Pop, Rock & Roll, Rap, Opera, and Enka.

Default songs: Dragon's Fire (Metal), Loving Things (Pop), Real Loud Bark (Rock & Roll), Livin' the Dream (Rap), Maybe, Baby (Ballad), The Heart's Descent (Opera), Operation Robodachi (Techno), and Reach for the Stars (Musical).

items.heading: What You Can Edit in a Performance
items.summary: Concert Hall customization goes beyond simply pressing play.
points: You can change a song's lyrics and title.
You can change the singer's outfit before the performance.
You can add extra Miis as background dancers.
This makes Concert Hall work both as a music feature and as a joke-making tool for custom island stories.

items.heading: How Group Performances Work
items.summary: Groups unlock once multiple Miis know the same song.
points: Groups can contain 2 to 7 Miis.
Every Mii in the group must know the same song before that song can be performed together.
The order in which Miis are placed affects their roles in the performance.
Songs like Metal and Rock & Roll use one main vocalist with the others as instrumentalists; songs like Pop and Musical have all members sing.
The game allows up to 50 groups and 50 saved group songs.

items.heading: Why Concert Hall Is Memorable
items.summary: Nintendo itself spotlighted custom Concert Hall performances.
points: Nintendo UK ran a 2015 Friendship Fiesta Concert Hall Contest built around player-made Tomodachi Life songs.
The official feature highlighted how custom lyrics and bizarre performance energy made Concert Hall one of the game's most shareable systems.
For creative players, Concert Hall is a strong mix of music utility and fan-favorite island storytelling.

tomodachiLifeVoiceCatchphrases.title: Tomodachi Life Voice and Catchphrases Guide
tomodachiLifeVoiceCatchphrases.subtitle: Tune how Miis sound, how names are pronounced, and what they say when happy, sad, angry, worried, or just being themselves.
tomodachiLifeVoiceCatchphrases.intro: Tomodachi Life sells the illusion of personality through text-to-speech, speech bubbles, and player-written phrases. A Mii's voice settings, spoken name data, catchphrase, and emotional lines all work together to make apartment dialogue feel personal.

items.title: Voice Sliders and Preset Voices
items.summary: Every Mii can use a preset, a random voice, or a fully customized voice.
points: 6 preset voices: male child, female child, male adult, female adult, elderly male, and elderly female.
Custom voices use 6 sliders: Pitch, Speed, Quality, Tone, Accent, and Intonation.
Pitch controls how high or low the voice sounds; Speed controls how quickly the Mii talks.
Quality controls how clear the speech sounds; Tone affects how high-pitched the voice is at the start of a sentence.
Accent affects how low-pitched the voice is at the end of a sentence; Intonation changes the sentence contour.

items.title: Name Pronunciation System
items.summary: Tomodachi Life separates written names from spoken pronunciation.
points: First name and last name allow up to 15 written characters; nickname allows up to 10.
Pronunciation can be entered separately from the visible name.
Pronunciation fields allow up to 30 characters for first and last name, and 20 for nickname.
This pronunciation entry feature is absent in the Japanese version.
This system lets players fix unusual spellings or joke names without changing the displayed name.

items.title: Five Phrase Slots for Everyday Mood
items.summary: Miis have five major phrase slots tied to everyday mood and problem states.
points: The five phrase types are: Catchphrase, Happy phrase, Sad phrase, Angry phrase, and Worried phrase.
A catchphrase is given as a level-up reward.
The four emotional phrases are given when a Mii has a related problem state.
In Tomodachi Life, once those emotional phrases have been given, they can be edited later while the Mii is awake.
In international versions, each phrase type has 8 accompanying animations, and any animation can be assigned to any phrase type.

items.title: Apartment Speech Bubbles and Random Dialogue
items.summary: The apartment entrance bubble is where a lot of the game's personality lands.
points: When you enter a Mii's apartment, they may say their catchphrase, a random line, or a mood line tied to being sick, worried, sad, happy, or angry.
If the Mii has no active problem, you will see a normal speech bubble with an ellipsis.
Tapping that bubble makes the Mii say a random dialogue line.
After tapping the speech bubble, a new one appears every hour.

tomodachiLifeUnlockGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Unlock Guide
tomodachiLifeUnlockGuide.subtitle: Follow the fastest clean path to open stores, social systems, family housing, and StreetPass locations.
tomodachiLifeUnlockGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life unlocks its island in layers. Population size, solved problems, money saved, level-up gifts, romance events, marriage, and StreetPass activity all push the island forward.

steps.title: Start with the Built-In Basics
steps.description: Your island begins with core everyday locations already open. The Fountain is tied to the opening tutorial flow.

steps.title: Grow the Island and Solve Early Problems
steps.description: The first meaningful expansion comes from adding both male and female Miis and clearing early apartment requests.
unlocks: Mii News: 7 islanders with at least 3M + 3F

steps.title: Use Money, Level-Ups, and Treasures
steps.description: This phase unlocks core support buildings for interiors, music, rankings, compatibility checks, and selling valuables.
unlocks: Concert Hall: give a song to a leveled Mii
Observation Tower: give a gift to a leveled Mii

steps.title: Hit the Midgame Population Milestone
steps.description: A larger island opens up more management and content tools.
unlocks: Nintendo 3DS Image Share: take a photo of a Mii apartment

steps.title: Push Romance Forward
steps.description: Once confession events start happening, several leisure areas open together.
unlocks: Park: have an islander confess love
Amusement Park: have an islander confess love
Café: have a Mii confess love for another Mii

steps.title: Finish the Family and Travel Layer
steps.description: Marriage and StreetPass activity open the late-game family and traveler systems.
unlocks: Mii Homes: celebrate an islander wedding
Port: activate StreetPass + visit Hat Store first
Import Wear: StreetPass, SpotPass, or export quiz trigger
Campground: send a traveler or host an arriving traveler

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Tomodachi Life

questions.question: What is Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream?
questions.answer: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is the upcoming Nintendo Switch sequel to the 3DS classic. It releases on April 16, 2026, supports up to 2 players, and includes a free downloadable demo available now.

questions.question: Is there a Tomodachi Life demo available?
questions.answer: Yes. A free demo for Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is available to download on Nintendo Switch right now. The demo lets you start building your island before the full game launches.

questions.question: How many languages does Tomodachi Life support?
questions.answer: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream supports 10 languages: Japanese, English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Korean, Dutch, Chinese Simplified, and Chinese Traditional.

questions.question: How do Mii personalities work in Tomodachi Life?
questions.answer: During Mii creation, four sliders place each resident into one of 16 personality types grouped as Easygoing, Outgoing, Independent, and Confident. Personalities affect social chemistry, dialogue flavor, and romance outcomes.

questions.question: How do I get QR codes for Tomodachi Life?
questions.answer: You can find Mii QR codes on MiiWiki's official archive, the Crystal Dreams community page, and Nintendo Life's curated packs. Scan them in Mii Maker, then import through Town Hall's New Mii option.

questions.question: What is the best beginner tip for Tomodachi Life?
questions.answer: Prioritize solving black-bubble problem requests as soon as they appear. Each solved problem unlocks new buildings, expands your island, and creates more social events between residents.

cta.title: Tomodachi Life를 탐험할 준비가 되셨나요?
cta.description: Mii QR Code, 팁, 토론을 위해 Reddit의 Tomodachi Life 커뮤니티에 참여하세요!

footer.title: Tomodachi Life 위키
footer.description: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream 가이드, Mii 성격, QR Code, 인간관계, 섬 생활 팁을 위한 완벽한 리소스 허브입니다.
footer.copyright: © 2026 Tomodachi Life 위키. Nintendo와 관련이 없습니다.

release.title: Tomodachi Life 출시
release.description: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream 출시일, 예약 구매 특전, 가격, 패키지 버전 가용성에 관한 모든 것. 최신 출시 소식을 확인하세요.
release.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life 출시일 및 예약 구매 가이드
release.metaDescription: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream 출시일, 예약 구매 특전, 가격 및 패키지 상세 정보에 대한 전체 가이드. 최신 출시 소식을 받아보세요.
release.whatIs: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream이란 무엇인가요?
release.whatIsDescription: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream은 Nintendo 3DS용 원작 Tomodachi Life의 기대를 모으는 후속작입니다. 새로운 기능, 콘텐츠, 소셜 경험과 함께 매력적인 Mii 생활 시뮬레이션을 Nintendo Switch로 가져옵니다.

whySteps.step1Description: 패키지 또는 디지털 버전을 확보하기 위해 언제 어디서 예약 구매를 해야 하는지 정확히 파악하세요.
whySteps.step2Description: 조기 구매 시 제공되는 독점 특전과 DLC가 무엇인지 알아보세요.
whySteps.step3Description: 출시일, 지역별 출시, 다운로드 용량에 대한 정보를 확인하세요.

demo.title: Tomodachi Life Demo
demo.description: 다운로드 방법, 이용 가능한 콘텐츠, 초기화를 통한 재시작 방법을 포함한 Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Demo 전체 가이드.
demo.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Demo 가이드 - 다운로드 및 초기화
demo.metaDescription: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Demo에 관한 모든 것. 다운로드 방법, 이용 가능한 기능, 새로운 플레이를 위한 Demo 초기화 방법을 알아보세요.
demo.whatIsDescription: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Demo는 Nintendo eShop에서 이용 가능한 무료 체험판입니다. 정식 출시 전에 섬 생활의 주요 기능, Mii 상호작용, 핵심 게임플레이를 경험할 수 있습니다.

whySteps.step1Description: 부담 없이 핵심적인 Mii 섬 게임플레이를 시도해 보세요.
whySteps.step2Description: 출시 전에 조작법, 일과, 핵심 메커니즘을 익히세요.
whySteps.step3Description: Demo를 초기화하고 여러 번 탐색하는 방법을 알아보세요.

switch.title: Tomodachi Life Switch
switch.description: Living the Dream 게임플레이 기능, Switch 독점 콘텐츠, 원작 3DS 버전과의 비교를 포함한 Nintendo Switch용 Tomodachi Life에 대한 모든 상세 정보.
switch.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Switch - Living the Dream 기능 및 가이드
switch.metaDescription: Nintendo Switch용 Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream 전체 가이드. 새로운 기능, 게임플레이 변경 사항, Switch 버전만의 특별한 점을 확인하세요.
switch.whatIsDescription: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream은 사랑받는 Mii 섬 생활 시뮬레이션을 Nintendo Switch로 가져옵니다. Switch 버전은 원작 3DS 타이틀에는 없던 온라인 기능, 확장된 커스터마이징, 새로운 소셜 메커니즘을 도입했습니다.

whySteps.step1Description: 향상된 그래픽, 온라인 연결성, Switch 독점 콘텐츠를 즐기세요.
whySteps.step2Description: 다른 플레이어의 섬을 방문하고 전 세계적으로 Mii QR Code를 공유하세요.
whySteps.step3Description: Switch 버전 전용의 새로운 이벤트, 아이템, 상호작용을 경험하세요.

personality.title: Tomodachi Life 성격
personality.description: 모든 Tomodachi Life 성격 유형, 성격 차트, Mii 간의 궁합, 성격 특성이 섬의 관계에 미치는 영향에 대한 전체 가이드.
personality.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life 성격 가이드 - 모든 유형 및 차트
personality.metaDescription: 모든 성격 유형, 성격 차트, 궁합, 특성이 Mii의 행동을 어떻게 형성하는지 다루는 전체 가이드를 통해 Tomodachi Life 성격을 마스터하세요.
personality.whatIsDescription: Tomodachi Life는 Mii가 상호작용하고, 우정을 쌓고, 사랑에 빠지는 방식을 결정하는 여러 가지 뚜렷한 유형의 성격 시스템을 사용합니다. 각 성격 유형은 대화, 취미, 다른 Mii와의 궁합에 영향을 미칩니다.
personality.why: 왜 Tomodachi Life 성격에 대해 알아야 하나요?

whySteps.step1Description: 우정을 장려하기 위해 어떤 성격 유형이 서로 가장 잘 어울리는지 이해하세요.
whySteps.step2Description: Mii 커플이 탄생할 확률을 높이기 위해 궁합이 맞는 성격을 매칭하세요.
whySteps.step3Description: 가장 재미있는 상호작용을 만들어내는 성격 조합을 선택하세요.

mii.title: Tomodachi Life Mii
mii.description: QR Code, Mii 수용 한도, 페이스 페인팅 옵션, 완벽한 섬 주민을 만들기 위한 팁을 포함한 Tomodachi Life Mii에 관한 모든 것.
mii.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Mii 가이드 - QR Code, 페이스 페인팅 등
mii.metaDescription: QR Code, Mii 제한, 페이스 페인팅 커스터마이징, 최고의 섬 출연진 구성을 위한 팁을 다루는 전체 Tomodachi Life Mii 가이드.
mii.whatIsDescription: Mii는 Tomodachi Life의 중심이 되는 커스터마이징 가능한 캐릭터입니다. 고유한 얼굴, 성격, 목소리를 가진 Mii를 생성하거나 QR Code를 통해 가져오고 커스터마이징할 수 있습니다. 각 Mii는 섬에 살며 독특한 이벤트와 이야기를 만들어냅니다.
mii.why: 왜 Tomodachi Life Mii를 마스터해야 하나요?

whySteps.step1Description: QR Code를 사용하여 좋아하는 캐릭터를 가져오거나 처음부터 직접 만드세요.
whySteps.step2Description: Mii를 적절히 섞으면 더 흥미로운 관계와 스토리가 만들어집니다.
whySteps.step3Description: 페이스 페인팅, 목소리, 외모를 커스터마이징하여 각 Mii를 독특하게 만드세요.

mod.title: Tomodachi Life 모드
mod.description: Tomodachi Life 모드, 세이브 에디터, 에뮬레이터 설정, ROM 정보에 대한 가이드. 커스터마이징 옵션과 Tomodachi Life 경험을 확장하는 방법을 탐색하세요.
mod.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life 모드, 세이브 에디터 및 에뮬레이터 가이드
mod.metaDescription: Tomodachi Life 모드, 세이브 에디터, 에뮬레이터 설정, ROM 상세 정보에 대한 전체 가이드. 게임을 커스터마이징하고 전체 모딩 커뮤니티를 탐색하세요.
mod.whatIsDescription: Tomodachi Life 모드와 세이브 에디터를 사용하면 표준 옵션 이상으로 게임을 커스터마이징할 수 있습니다. 텍스처 교체부터 세이브 편집 도구 및 에뮬레이터 설정에 이르기까지, 모딩 커뮤니티는 경험을 향상하고 개인화할 수 있는 수많은 방법을 만들었습니다.

whySteps.step1Description: 기본 게임에서는 사용할 수 없는 콘텐츠와 커스터마이징에 접근하세요.
whySteps.step2Description: 에뮬레이터를 사용하여 PC나 다른 기기에서 Tomodachi Life를 즐기세요.
whySteps.step3Description: 세이브 에디터 도구로 섬 데이터, Mii 능력치, 자원을 수정하세요.

guide.title: Tomodachi Life 가이드
guide.description: 돈을 빨리 버는 방법, Mii를 사랑에 빠지게 하는 방법, 염색약 얻는 방법, 섬 이름 짓기, 섬 생활의 모든 측면을 마스터하는 방법을 다루는 전체 Tomodachi Life 가이드.
guide.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life 가이드 - 팁, 돈, 로맨스 등
guide.metaDescription: 돈 벌기, 로맨스 메커니즘, 아이템 위치, 섬 이름 짓기, 모든 최고의 게임플레이 팁을 다루는 종합 가이드로 Tomodachi Life를 마스터하세요.
guide.whatIsDescription: Tomodachi Life 가이드는 돈 벌기와 로맨스 구축부터 희귀 아이템 찾기와 완벽한 섬 이름 짓기까지 게임의 모든 측면을 다룹니다. 이 가이드들은 플레이어가 Tomodachi Life 경험을 최대한 활용할 수 있도록 돕습니다.

whySteps.step1Description: 돈을 빨리 벌고 프리미엄 아이템을 구매할 수 있는 검증된 전략을 배우세요.
whySteps.step2Description: Mii 관계와 결혼 뒤에 숨겨진 메커니즘을 이해하세요.
whySteps.step3Description: Mii를 행복하게 유지하기 위해 염색약이나 특별한 선물 같은 희귀 아이템의 위치를 찾으세요.
